- Add priority_demo.cpp example

commit.message:
Revert async CSS loading to fix CLS 0.394 regression

The critical CSS + async loading approach caused layout shifts when
full CSS loaded, dropping PageSpeed from 90-98% to 72-79%.
Restores render-blocking CSS import (now only 21 KiB gzip after PurgeCSS).

fix: correct default VISION_URL from twomiah-vision to home-visualizer

The fallback URL was pointing to twomiah-vision.onrender.com which
doesn't exist. The actual service is home-visualizer.onrender.com.
This caused the visualizer iframe to 404 on deployed tenant sites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: marks agent model mismatch + cascade only from infra steps

- Marks was configured with model google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview but
  routed to DeepSeek provider which can't serve it (400 Bad Request on
  every run). Changed to deepseek-chat.
- Cascade-fail logic was too aggressive: any single agent failure would
  mark all other pending agents as failed for that ticker. Now only
  cascades from infrastructure steps (data_fetch, data_validate,
  pre_filter, gate_decision) so agent failures are independent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(proxy): implement full Anthropic↔OpenAI protocol conversion with tool support

Rewrites protocol_converter.go to address all P0 gaps identified in the
protocol conversion design document (docs/protocol_conversion_design.md):

Request conversion (Anthropic → OpenAI):
- assistant tool_use blocks → OpenAI tool_calls array (input object → arguments JSON string)
- user tool_result blocks → role:tool messages (split from user message)
- mixed tool_result + text → tool messages first, user text message last
- tools definition: input_schema → parameters, wrapped in {type:function, function:{...}}
- tool_choice: any→required, auto→auto, none→none, tool→{type:function,...}
- disable_parallel_tool_use → parallel_tool_calls:false

Response conversion (OpenAI → Anthropic):
- Non-streaming: tool_calls → tool_use content blocks (arguments JSON string → input object)
- finish_reason: tool_calls/function_call → tool_use
- usage split: input_tokens = prompt_tokens - cached_tokens,
  cache_read_input_tokens = cached_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens = 0

Streaming conversion (buffered strategy):
- Buffer all OpenAI SSE chunks until [DONE] before emitting Anthropic events
- Ensures message_start carries accurate input_tokens (only available at end of stream)
- Streaming tool calls: tool_use content blocks with input_json_delta events
- Parallel tool calls supported via index-keyed buffers with insertion-order output

Adds 30 new tests covering all P0 scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: レビュー指摘 Critical 3件 + Important 2件を修正

- is_branch_merged: 未分岐ワーカー(HEAD==origin/main)の false-positive を防止
  rev-list で local commits が 0 なら「未マージ」扱いに
- capture_dirty_diff: 手動 diff 生成 → git diff --no-index に置換
  binary ファイル・no-newline ファイルを正しく処理
- validate_worker_name: denylist → allowlist [a-zA-Z0-9_-] に変更
  shell injection 文字を根本的にブロック
- apply_patch: stderr をキャプチャしてエラーメッセージに含める
- leading hyphen/underscore の名前を拒否

Full-stack Leptos 0.8 app with SSR + WASM hydration for streaming
sacred audio tracks from Guruji Sivananda. Includes PostgreSQL-backed
track management with full-text search, S3/local audio storage,

offline audio caching via service worker.

Co-authored-by: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: standardize role dependencies and fix configuration issues (#28) - Add meta/main.yml with proper dependencies to all roles that were missing them (apps, neovim, raycast, ssh, zed) - Add missing xdg dependency to tmux role - Replace redundant "Verify Homebrew" tasks with homebrew role dependency across all roles - Remove redundant conf.d directory creation from starship and mise roles — now guaranteed by zsh role dependency chain - Change claude-code, mise, and starship meta dependencies from xdg to zsh (which transitively depends on xdg) since they write to zsh/conf.d - Replace hardcoded macbook SSH key with dynamic ansible_hostname lookup via new op_machine_ssh_key variable - Fix op_greptile_tokens.personal.vault pointing to "Work" instead of "Personal" - Remove stale op_vault default variable (no "Dotfiles" vault exists) - Add per-entry vault fields to all 1Password token references - Fill in git identity placeholders with real values and signing keys - Rename berkeley_mono_repo to fonts_repo with correct GitHub path Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

Greptile Summary

This PR standardizes role dependency management across the Ansible playbook by adding `meta/main.yml` files to roles that were missing them, replacing redundant inline Homebrew verification tasks with proper role dependencies, and migrating from a global `op_vault` default to per-entry vault fields for all 1Password lookups. It also fills in real git identity values, renames `berkeley_mono_repo` to `fonts_repo`, and dynamically resolves the machine SSH key using `ansible_hostname`. Key issues found: - **`roles/fonts/tasks/main.yml`**: The git clone `dest` is set to `{{ xdg_data_home }}` (e.g., `~/.local/share`) rather than `{{ xdg_data_home }}/fonts`. The `ansible.builtin.git` module will fail because `xdg_data_home` is already a non-empty directory, and even if it didn't fail it would pollute the XDG data home root with `.git` metadata. - **`roles/graphite/templates/user_config.j2`**: The required top-level `authToken` field was removed and all profiles are now placed under `alternativeProfiles` only. Graphite CLI expects a primary `authToken` at the top level for the active profile — this change will break Graphite authentication. - **`roles/raycast/tasks/main.yml`**: The `---` YAML document start marker was accidentally dropped when removing the Homebrew guard block.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- Not safe to merge — two logic bugs will cause runtime failures in the fonts and graphite roles. - The majority of this PR is a clean, well-structured refactor. However, two concrete runtime failures are introduced: the fonts repo clone destination is incorrect and will fail against a non-empty directory, and the Graphite config template removes the required primary `authToken` field which will break `gt` authentication. - `roles/fonts/tasks/main.yml` (incorrect clone dest) and `roles/graphite/templates/user_config.j2` (missing primary authToken).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | group_vars/all.yml | Fills in git identity and SSH signing keys (public keys only — no secrets), renames `berkeley_mono_repo` to `fonts_repo`, replaces the global `op_vault` default with per-entry vault fields across all token entries, fixes the Greptile personal vault pointing to "Work", and extracts the machine SSH key into a dedicated `op_machine_ssh_key` variable using `ansible_hostname`. | | roles/fonts/tasks/main.yml | Critical bug: `dest` for the git clone is set to `{{ xdg_data_home }}` instead of `{{ xdg_data_home }}/fonts`. This will attempt to clone the fonts repo into the XDG data home root, which is already a non-empty directory, causing the task to fail. The find path (`{{ xdg_data_home }}/fonts`) is also inconsistent with the clone destination. | | roles/graphite/templates/user_config.j2 | Removes the required top-level `authToken` field from the Graphite config; all profiles are now placed under `alternativeProfiles` only. Graphite CLI requires a primary `authToken` at the top level — without it, authentication will fail. | | roles/ssh/tasks/main.yml | Adds two new tasks to provision the machine SSH key (private and public) using the dynamic `op_machine_ssh_key` variable and `ansible_hostname`. Removes the `vault | default(op_vault)` fallback since per-entry vaults are now required. Clean change. | | roles/ssh/templates/config.j2 | Replaces the hardcoded `id_ed25519_macbook` identity file reference with `id_ed25519_{{ ansible_hostname }}`, making the SSH config portable across machines. | | roles/claude-code/tasks/main.yml | Dramatically simplified — removes MCP server config, skill deployment, settings/keybindings, and plugin marketplace setup. Now only creates the XDG-compliant config directory and sets `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` via a zsh conf.d snippet. | | roles/graphite/tasks/main.yml | Switches 1Password vault lookup from global `op_vault` default to per-entry `op_graphite_tokens[item].vault`. Clean and consistent with the rest of the refactor. | | roles/raycast/tasks/main.yml | Removes Homebrew verification block and updates state to `latest`. Accidentally dropped the `---` YAML document start marker in the process. | | roles/tmux/templates/tmux.conf.j2 | Replaces `tmux-sensible` with `tmux-resurrect`, enables 1-based window indexing, clipboard integration, vi-mode copy, and vim-style pane navigation keybindings. Safe configuration additions. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `roles/graphite/templates/user_config.j2`, line 1-10 ([link](https://github.com/theseifhassan/dotfiles/blob/e4938446efd01c60661c3b4488d631d2e1fc22a8/roles/graphite/templates/user_config.j2#L1-L10)) **Removal of top-level `authToken` likely breaks Graphite authentication** The Graphite CLI config schema expects a top-level `authToken` field to identify the primary/active profile. The previous template set `authToken` to the first identity profile's token, with remaining profiles listed under `alternativeProfiles`. This PR removes the top-level `authToken` entirely and puts all profiles—including what was previously the primary—into `alternativeProfiles` only. Without a top-level `authToken`, Graphite will not have an active profile and authentication will fail for the primary context.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: roles/graphite/templates/user_config.j2 Line: 1-10 Comment: **Removal of top-level `authToken` likely breaks Graphite authentication** The Graphite CLI config schema expects a top-level `authToken` field to identify the primary/active profile. The previous template set `authToken` to the first identity profile's token, with remaining profiles listed under `alternativeProfiles`. This PR removes the top-level `authToken` entirely and puts all profiles—including what was previously the primary—into `alternativeProfiles` only. Without a top-level `authToken`, Graphite will not have an active profile and authentication will fail for the primary context. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

commit.message:
feat: ControlPanel as AvalonDock dockable, Views ribbon panel toggles

- ControlPanel을 AvalonDock LayoutAnchorable로 변환 (도킹/플로팅 지원)
- ControlPanelView 커스텀 타이틀바 제거 (AvalonDock 타이틀바 단일화)
- Views 리본탭 DriveTree 토글 → 좌측 트리패널 Show/Hide 연동
- Views 리본탭 Main Panel 토글 → 파라메터 영역 확장/축소 양방향 동기화
